specification.topic-statement: The command dispatcher routes slash-prefixed input lines to registered command handlers by exact name match and persists successful command output to the database.

scope.in-scope: Command recognition, routing, argument extraction, the complete command registry, echo and output formatting, database persistence of command output, and error handling for unknown commands.
scope.boundary: Partial command name completion is performed by the tab completion subsystem before the user submits the input. The dispatcher only sees complete, submitted input and performs no prefix matching.
What each command does — how it modifies agent state, what it outputs — is outside the scope of this specification.

field.name: name
field.note: exact command name without the leading slash

field.name: handler
field.note: signature: (allocator context, REPL context, args string or null) → result

behavior.command-registry:
The registry contains exactly 18 commands in a fixed ordered list:
| Name | Purpose | |------|---------| | `clear` | Clear scrollback, session messages, and marks | | `mark` | Create a rollback checkpoint, optionally named | | `rewind` | Roll back to a checkpoint, optionally by name | | `fork` | Create a child agent | | `kill` | Terminate an agent | | `send` | Send a message to another agent | | `wait` | Block until messages arrive from other agents | | `reap` | Remove dead agents | | `agents` | Display the agent process tree | | `help` | List available commands | | `model` | Change the active LLM model and thinking level | | `system` | Set or display the system prompt | | `tool` | List tools or show a tool's schema | | `refresh` | Reload the tool registry from disk | | `pin` | Manage pinned document paths | | `unpin` | Remove a pinned document path | | `toolset` | Restrict which tools are visible to the LLM | | `exit` | Quit the application |
The list is static and never modified at runtime.
behavior.input-parsing:
Input is recognized as a command when it begins with a forward slash (`/`). The dispatcher:
1. Strips the leading `/`. 2. Skips any leading whitespace. 3. Extracts the command name: all characters up to the first whitespace or end of string. 4. Skips whitespace between the command name and the arguments. 5. Treats the remainder as the arguments string. If no text remains, the arguments are absent (null, not an empty string).
An input of `/` alone (no command name) is rejected with an error.
behavior.routing:
The command name is compared against each registry entry using a case-sensitive exact string match. The list is scanned from the first entry to the last. The first match is used.
If no entry matches, a warning is appended to the scrollback: `Unknown command 'name'`, and an invalid-argument error is returned. The input is not persisted to the database on an unknown command.
behavior.echo-and-output:
Before invoking the handler, the original input line (including the leading `/`) is echoed to the scrollback in a dark-gray color, followed by a blank line.
After the handler returns successfully, if the handler appended at least one line to the scrollback, an additional blank line is appended. Handlers that produce no output do not receive a trailing blank line.
The echo always appears; it is not conditional on handler success or output.
behavior.database-persistence:
After a handler returns successfully, the command is persisted to the database as a `command` event. The persisted record contains:
- The full original input line as the echo field. - Any lines the handler appended to the scrollback during execution as the output content. - The command name and arguments in a structured data field.
Failed commands (handlers that return an error) are not persisted.
If the database write itself fails, the error is logged but the failure is not returned to the caller; the command is considered to have completed successfully from the user's perspective.
behavior.handler-contract:
Each handler receives: - A talloc allocator context for temporary allocations. - The REPL context for accessing agent state, scrollback, and the database. - The arguments string (null if no arguments were supplied).
Handlers return a result indicating success or failure. Failures are displayed to the user by appending a warning to the scrollback before the handler returns, not by the dispatcher after the fact.
